title: StreamSentry: The High-Throughput Log Processor
description: Build a production-grade log processing tool that reads massive server logs (simulated as 100MB+ files), filters for critical errors, and calculates real-time statistics without exceeding a 50MB memory limit.
requirements: Implement a Readable Stream to read a large text file in chunks.
Create a custom Transform Stream that filters lines containing the keyword 'ERROR' and 'CRITICAL'.
A Writable Stream that writes the filtered 'Critical' logs into a new file.
A real-time counter that tracks the number of errors per second using the 'data' event.
Integration of the 'zlib' module to compress the output file on the fly.
Ensure the total memory usage of the process stays below 50MB regardless of input file size.

milestones.title: Basic Pumping
tasks: Set up a basic pipe from a read stream to a write stream
Generate a mock 100MB log file for testing

milestones.title: The Filter Engine
tasks: Implement the Transform stream to detect keywords
Add a line-by-line processing logic using the 'readline' module or custom chunk splitting

milestones.title: Optimization & Compression
tasks: Pipe the filtered stream into a Gzip compressor
Implement the stats tracker to count errors in real-time

stretchGoals: Implement a 'Backpressure' mechanism to handle a slow writable stream without crashing.
Create a Worker Thread that calculates the hash of the log file in parallel to the streaming process.
Build a simple HTTP server that allows users to trigger a new log scan via an API endpoint.

evaluationRubric.memoryEfficiency: Does the process maintain a constant memory footprint regardless of file size?
evaluationRubric.throughput: How many MBs per second can the engine process?
evaluationRubric.correctness: Are all 'CRITICAL' logs captured and correctly compressed?
